mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) like Kimi-K2.5 and Gemma. It provides a straightforward way to run and manage models via a command-line interface and REST API. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the need for a Kubernetes environment for scaling and orchestration.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application on your Kubernetes cluster using the official Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/helm

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Ollama deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment ollama --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace ollama

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/monitoring.yaml --namespace ollama

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Deploy monitoring resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the Ollama deployment.
> This command deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This process will upgrade the Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods --namespace ollama

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> This command upgrades the Ollama deployment.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama image may not be available or accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have access to the Docker registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace ollama' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Ollama deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests in the deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama service may not be running or exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'kubectl get svc --namespace ollama' and ensure it is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and improved REST API endpoints.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' when running 'kubectl get nodes'. The Ollama service should also be accessible via the configured endpoints.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
